name: @webspinner-foundation/database-application
description: Takes a Webspinner's plain-English description of what they want to keep track of, researches the domain from credible public references, proposes the full set of screens (forms, lists, details, reports) a competent professional would build for that domain, offers three branding palettes (warm / cool / Foundation default), iterates as the patron prunes and chooses, then builds the working web application — schema derived from the screens, collections in the patron's Cell, branding applied at runtime. Bookkeeping, plant tracking, donor logging, customer service: same Spinner, different domain. The schema is engineering, hidden from the patron; the screens are theirs to design.

capabilities.name: propose
capabilities.description: First turn — the Spinner reads the patron's sentence, researches the domain, proposes the full set of screens a working professional would build (forms / lists / details / reports), offers three branding palettes, and asks focused clarifying questions naming screen-level decisions. Saves session state at phase 'proposed'.

patronSentence.description: The Webspinner's plain-English description of what they want to keep track of.

narration.description: Patron-readable Markdown — what the Spinner consulted (with source URLs), what it sketched, and why.

domain.description: The Spinner's identification of the domain (e.g. 'small-business bookkeeping', 'home gardening').

screensDraft.description: The drafted screens (forms, lists, details, reports), their navigation, and the three branding palette options. Inspectable; the patron prunes via clarifications.

branding.description: Branding state — `options` (three palette proposals), `selectedPaletteId` (null in propose; set in refine), optional `customDescription` / `referenceUrl`.

clarifications.description: Focused questions, each naming the screen or field its answer modifies. One question is always the branding choice (palette selection / custom description / website reference).

id.description: Stable identifier the next call references in its `answers` array.

capabilities.name: refine
capabilities.description: Subsequent turns — applies the patron's answers as concrete screen-level deltas (adds/removes screens, sections, fields), settles on the branding choice, and either asks more screen-level questions or marks the design ready to build. Saves session state at phase 'refining' or 'ready'.

answers.description: The patron's answers to the prior turn's clarifications. Each answer references a clarification id and carries the patron's response.

readyToBuild.description: True when the schema is settled and the next call should be `build`. False while clarifications remain.

capabilities.name: build
capabilities.description: Final turn — reads the settled screens + branding from session state, derives the schema from the screens (form fields → columns, link-to fields → relations), creates the Cell-side collections, writes the screens + branding to wp_database_applications, and returns the surface URL the patron lands on. Saves session state at phase 'built' with status 'completed'.

deployedSurfaceUrl.description: The route inside the Loom where the patron's application is reachable.

artifacts.description: The concrete things the Spinner produced — collections, view bindings, report definitions.
